meaning: vt.居住于,存在于,占据,栖息
examples: They inhabit reed huts built on stilts above the water.
The Olympic marmots inhabit meadows high in the Olympic Mountains where the weather conditions are much harsher.
A similar strategy—accelerated development combined with a resting stage—has also allowed amphibians to inhabit deserts.
Fox (Meryl Streep), and their son, Ash (Jason Schwartzman), inhabit a world that's disarmingly, well, lifelike.
The social network has helped create the buzzing hive of information that we all inhabit.
